when-stuck.name: Stack Overflow
when-stuck.why: Still the default Q&A archive with 24M+ questions indexed
when-stuck.last_active_signal: 2026 dev survey live; hourly new questions
when-stuck.review: Traffic declined post-ChatGPT but the answer archive remains authoritative for JS/Python/Rust/TS. No card ever. Signup only needed to ask/answer.

when-stuck.name: DEV Community
when-stuck.why: Active Forem-powered dev blog + Q&A network
when-stuck.review: 3.97M members, lighter-weight than SO for tutorials and opinion pieces. Fully free, no card.

when-stuck.name: Reddit r/experienceddevs
when-stuck.review: Highest-signal senior-dev sub. r/programming and r/learnprogramming also worth following. Free.

when-stuck.name: Exercism
when-stuck.why: Free mentored practice in 83 languages, donation-funded
when-stuck.review: 100% free forever, nonprofit. 8,521+ exercises, human mentor review. Matches our donations-only rule perfectly.

when-stuck.name: Stack Exchange Network
when-stuck.review: Superuser, ServerFault, Unix.SE, DBA.SE, Code Review. Free.

when-stuck.name: Software Engineering SE
when-stuck.why: Design/architecture Q&A where SO closes as opinion-based
when-stuck.review: Conceptual Q&A companion to Stack Overflow's implementation focus.

when-stuck.name: devRant
when-stuck.review: Alive despite the rumors. Low volume vs 2020 peak. Free.

when-stuck.name: Coderanch
when-stuck.why: Old-school moderated Java forum still active in niches

when-stuck.name: Discord dev communities
when-stuck.why: Real-time help across Rust, Python, Reactiflux, framework servers
when-stuck.review: Not a stuck-help site itself but hosts thousands of dev communities. Best for real-time help. Free.

news.name: Hacker News
news.review: No signup to read. Anti-corporate, high-signal front page. Free.

news.name: Changelog
news.review: Go Time, JS Party, Ship It, Practical AI. Ad-supported, free.

news.name: ACM TechNews
news.review: Confirmed alive; 403s to bots but publishing normally. Free email digest.

news.name: Ars Technica
news.review: Free tier readable; subscription optional for ad-free. Higher depth than Verge/CNET.

news.name: The Register
news.review: Blocks AI training bots, allows human readers. Free, ad-supported.

news.name: InfoQ
news.review: Free content plentiful; paid certs optional. Enterprise-software focused.

news.name: Hacker Newsletter
news.review: 60K+ readers, zero paywall. Skim-friendly alternative to doomscrolling HN.

news.name: The Pragmatic Engineer
news.review: Core paywalled ($15/mo). Free tier ~1 issue/month. Marginal on card-free rule; keep free-tier only.

learning.name: freeCodeCamp
learning.last_active_signal: 2026-05 Full Stack capstone; Rust rewrite March 2026
learning.review: Best-in-class free learning. 12+ certs, ~1,800 hours. Matches donations-only rule.

learning.name: The Odin Project
learning.review: No premium tier ever. Ruby on Rails + JS/Node paths. Emphasizes real dev workflows (Git, terminal, local setup).

learning.name: MDN Learn
learning.review: Core free, MDN Plus optional. Reference-plus-tutorial hybrid.

learning.name: roadmap.sh
learning.review: Community-driven on GitHub. Best orientation tool. No paywall.

learning.name: CS50 / edX Harvard
learning.why: Canonical CS intro, CS50-issued cert free
learning.review: 7.1M enrollments. Free cert available from CS50 directly (edX verified cert is paid).

learning.name: Fullstack Open
learning.why: University of Helsinki modern JS full-stack course
learning.review: Modern stack (Vite, Vitest, React Query). 5-14 ECTS credits available. Best free JS full-stack deep dive.

learning.name: boot.dev
learning.review: Content free to watch; interactive exercises gated ($49/mo). Passive learning genuinely free.

learning.name: Kaggle Learn
learning.review: 30h GPU + 20h TPU per week free. No card. Best zero-cost ML entry.

learning.name: Khan Academy
learning.review: No card ever. Beginner onboarding; less depth than freeCodeCamp/TOP.

learning.name: Fast.ai
learning.why: Top-down practical deep learning by Jeremy Howard
learning.review: 70-80 hours, PyTorch-based. Annually revised. Best free applied DL course.

learning.name: Learn Git Branching
learning.review: Client-side JS, no account. MIT, self-hostable. Unmatched for Git intuition.

learning.name: DeepLearning.AI
learning.why: Andrew Ng's short-course library, mostly free
learning.last_active_signal: 2026-05 AI Agents for Image/Video with Google Cloud
learning.review: 88+ short courses co-produced with OpenAI/Anthropic/Google/LangChain. Free short-course library alone is best free GenAI resource.

interview-prep.name: LeetCode
interview-prep.review: Free tier covers most FAANG DSA prep. Premium ($35/mo) unlocks company tags. Still standard for junior/mid despite AI-assisted interview shift.

interview-prep.name: NeetCode
interview-prep.why: Curated roadmaps that structure LeetCode's chaos
interview-prep.review: Free NeetCode 150 + Blind 75 + YouTube walkthroughs cover ~95% of patterns. Pro lifetime $219 one-time (no recurring card) is the card-free alternative to AlgoExpert.

interview-prep.name: System Design Primer
interview-prep.review: 296 open PRs backlog but base content remains the free canon. Anki flashcards included.

interview-prep.name: ByteByteGo
interview-prep.why: Alex Xu's visual system design content
interview-prep.review: YouTube channel + newsletter fully free — top free system-design content in 2026. Paid courses optional.

interview-prep.name: systemdesign.one
interview-prep.review: Neo Kim's article archive. Solid supplementary reading with clean diagrams.

interview-prep.name: Exponent
interview-prep.review: Absorbed Pramp fully by 2024. Peer mocks free after signup. Broader than SWE-only competitors.

interview-prep.name: HackerRank
interview-prep.review: Developer side remains strong free option. Question-bank leaks + 2026 ATS controversy dented recruiter trust.

interview-prep.name: Educative.io
interview-prep.review: 7-day trial no card. GitHub Student Pack unlocks 6mo free — meets card-free rule for students.

interview-prep.name: HiredInTech
interview-prep.review: Migrated to static; interactive grading gone but readable course material and system-design canvas remain.

tools.name: regex101
tools.why: Multi-flavor regex tester with match explanations
tools.review: PCRE2/JS/Python/Go/Java/.NET. Savable library with account. Privacy caveat: keystrokes sent server-side.

tools.name: JSONCrack
tools.review: Free web version; Premium $6/mo. Modular architecture in v5.

tools.name: diagrams.net / draw.io
tools.review: No Enterprise gating. Browser + desktop, no signup. Go-to for architecture sketches, C4.

tools.name: CyberChef
tools.why: Encoding/encryption Swiss army knife by GCHQ
tools.review: Open source, browser-only, data never leaves your machine.

tools.name: transform.tools
tools.review: JSON to TS/GraphQL/Zod/Go, SVG to JSX, HTML to Pug. Indispensable for one-off schema conversions.

tools.name: Excalidraw
tools.review: Free core enough for most work. Excalidraw+ optional cloud persistence.

tools.name: tldraw
tools.why: Free web whiteboard with instant collaboration
tools.review: Free canvas; SDK paid for commercial multiplayer embed.

tools.name: Compiler Explorer
tools.review: Community-supported. Indispensable for perf-critical code work.

tools.name: caniuse
tools.why: Browser support tables for HTML5/CSS/JS APIs
tools.review: Authoritative baseline for feature-detection decisions. Feeds shields.io, PostCSS.

tools.name: bundlephobia
tools.review: Still the standard pre-install check. Maintenance-mode-ish.

tools.name: pkg.pr.new
tools.why: Continuous npm preview releases per commit/PR
tools.review: StackBlitz-Labs maintained. Standard for library authors now.

tools.name: Ray.so
tools.why: Fresher Carbon alternative for code screenshots
tools.review: 17 themes, 50+ languages, PNG. Prefer over Carbon (dormant since Dec 2024).

tools.name: ShareX
tools.review: 80+ upload destinations. Best-in-class Windows tool for our corp-laptop context.

docs.name: MDN Web Docs
docs.review: Source of truth for browser-compat-data. Rock solid.

docs.name: DevDocs
docs.review: 100+ sources. MCP integration in 2026 lets agents query pinned versions.

docs.name: Rust Book
docs.why: Canonical Rust learning resource, ships with each release

docs.name: Astro docs
docs.review: Starlight-based, ships doc updates same day as releases. Rust compiler rewrite in progress.

docs.name: Cloudflare docs
docs.review: High signal for edge-first stack. Ships changelog per product.

docs.name: pnpm docs
docs.last_active_signal: pnpm 10 supported by CF Workers Builds 2026

docs.name: Tailwind docs
docs.why: Tailwind CSS v4 CSS-first config docs

docs.name: React docs (react.dev)
docs.review: Learn + Reference split with interactive sandboxes.

docs.name: Web.dev
docs.review: Prescriptive how-to on Core Web Vitals, PWAs. Complements MDN.

docs.name: Bun docs
docs.why: Fast all-in-one JS/TS toolkit powering Claude Code
docs.last_active_signal: v1.3.12 April 2026; Rust rewrite canary May 2026
docs.review: Powers Claude Code + Claude Agent SDK. Bun.markdown.ansi, headless browser automation.

competitive.name: Codeforces
competitive.review: Multiple rated rounds weekly. Explicit AI-use restrictions during main contests.

competitive.name: AtCoder
competitive.why: Japan-origin platform with weekly high-quality contests
competitive.review: ABC/ARC/AGC/AHC lineups. Strict AI rules on ARC/AGC.

competitive.name: CodeChef
competitive.review: Core contests free. Pivoted heavily toward student placement prep.

competitive.name: Kaggle competitions
competitive.last_active_signal: ARC Prize 2026 launching Nov 2026; multiple 2026 comps active
competitive.review: More active than ever in 2026. Free with GPU/TPU notebook quotas.

competitive.name: Project Euler
competitive.review: Removed leaderboard due to cheating, adding rolling rank table. Free.

competitive.name: Advent of Code
competitive.review: Scaled to 12 puzzles + no global leaderboard in 2025 due to AI solvers and DDoS. Private leaderboards still work. Free.

competitive.name: CSES Problem Set
competitive.why: Canonical structured problem set by Antti Laaksonen
competitive.review: Not a contest platform — the algorithm learning problem set. No ads, no card.

competitive.name: TopCoder
competitive.last_active_signal: MM 165 launched June 2026; AI Exponential League July 2026
competitive.review: Under Wipro. Pivoting to AI challenges vs classic SRMs. Community thinner than peak years.

competitive.name: HackerEarth
competitive.why: Community contests remain free for participants
competitive.last_active_signal: May 2026 release notes; Zerve AI Challenge June 2026
competitive.review: Firmly pivoted to B2B hiring/assessment. HourStorm + monthly circuits still free.

competitive.name: SPOJ
competitive.review: Maintenance mode — no major new contests since 2022. Still a valuable practice archive.

jobs.name: Hacker News Who's Hiring
jobs.review: Startup-heavy, YC-dominated. Signal-to-noise unmatched. Ephemeral format demands quick action.

jobs.name: hnhiring.com
jobs.why: Searchable index of HN Who's Hiring back to 2018
jobs.review: Filter by location/tech/remote. Makes ephemeral HN threads permanent.

jobs.name: Wellfound
jobs.review: Fully independent post-2022 rebrand. Direct founder access. Caveat: most applications expire unread.

jobs.name: Work At A Startup
jobs.why: YC's official board - single-profile apply to 1000+ YC startups
jobs.review: Unique access to funded companies pre-Series B. Founders reach out directly.

jobs.name: We Work Remotely
jobs.review: Real companies only, no recruiter noise. Free to browse and apply.

jobs.name: RemoteOK
jobs.review: Volume over curation. #OpenSalaries branding but most lack salary.

jobs.name: Welcome to the Jungle
jobs.review: Otta merged Jan 2024. Strong for UK/EU/London/NYC/SF.

jobs.name: 4dayweek.io
jobs.review: Niche but well-maintained. Useful filter if work-life balance is hard requirement.

jobs.name: Instahyre
jobs.review: Strong for engineers targeting Indian startups + FAANG India.

jobs.name: LinkedIn Jobs
jobs.review: Must-use for reach despite recruiter spam. AI-powered search in 2026.

blogs.name: Simon Willison
blogs.why: Best real-time source on generative-AI tooling
blogs.review: Multiple posts per day on LLMs, coding agents, Datasette, Python. Essential for AI/agent-era tracking.

blogs.name: Julia Evans
blogs.why: Legendary explainer of Unix, networking, Git, CSS
blogs.review: Gold standard for accessible systems writing. Monthly cadence.

blogs.name: Dan Abramov / overreacted.io
blogs.why: React thinker on atproto, RSC, Lean

blogs.name: Rachel by the Bay
blogs.why: SRE/systems war stories from ex-Facebook engineer
blogs.review: Terse, HN-front-page regular. Pairs with Dan Luu archive.

blogs.name: Fabien Sanglard
blogs.review: Meticulous, illustrated. Irreplaceable for game engine internals.

blogs.name: Xe Iaso
blogs.review: Essential for modern anti-scraping and Go infra.

blogs.name: Oxide Computer blog
blogs.review: Corporate but individual-authored. Rare production+rigor combo.

blogs.name: Marc Brooker
blogs.why: AWS principal on distributed systems + agent safety
blogs.review: Production scale + formal rigor + clear prose. Essential.

blogs.name: Josh Comeau
blogs.review: Low volume, reference-quality. Subgrid, linear(), animation-timeline API deep dives.

blogs.name: Drew DeVault
blogs.review: Opinionated but technically substantive. FOSS politics + systems.

blogs.name: Cassidy Williams
blogs.why: GitHub Sr DevRel director; warm accessible frontend + career

agent-era-2026.name: Anthropic Claude Docs
agent-era-2026.why: Canonical Claude/Anthropic API + Claude Code docs
agent-era-2026.review: docs.claude.com primary; docs.anthropic.com legacy alias. Essential for our CC stack.

agent-era-2026.name: Model Context Protocol
agent-era-2026.why: Canonical MCP spec, now Linux Foundation-governed
agent-era-2026.review: Latest spec at /specification/latest. Blog at blog.modelcontextprotocol.io.

agent-era-2026.name: agents.md
agent-era-2026.why: Open standard for repo-level agent instructions
agent-era-2026.review: OpenAI + Google + Cursor + Amp + Factory. AAIF governance (146 members). Our AGENTS.md files follow this.

agent-era-2026.name: agentskills.io
agent-era-2026.why: SKILL.md open format for progressive-disclosure skills
agent-era-2026.review: Backed by Agentic AI Foundation. Companion CLIs: skills.sh, skillshare, openskills.

agent-era-2026.name: Glama MCP
agent-era-2026.why: Largest verified MCP registry with quality grades
agent-era-2026.review: Superset of official registry. Every server verified + rebuilt + quality-scored.

agent-era-2026.name: Smithery
agent-era-2026.review: Registry free; hosted MCP paid. Registry API at smithery.ai/docs/registry.

agent-era-2026.name: awesome-mcp-servers
agent-era-2026.review: Referenced by mcp.so and Glama. Community-driven canon.

agent-era-2026.name: Cline
agent-era-2026.review: Apache 2.0. VS Code + CLI + JetBrains + Cursor + Windsurf + Zed + Neovim. ACP standard-holder.

agent-era-2026.name: OpenCode
agent-era-2026.review: By Anomaly (SST team). MIT. Windows-native. Displaced Claude Code as top-starred OSS agent.

agent-era-2026.name: Aider
agent-era-2026.why: Terminal AI pair programmer, git-native, model-agnostic
agent-era-2026.review: MIT, BYO API key. Runs anywhere with Python.

agent-era-2026.name: Ollama
agent-era-2026.why: Local LLM runner with auto GPU detection
agent-era-2026.review: Native Windows installer via winget. OpenAI-compatible endpoint.

agent-era-2026.name: Groq
agent-era-2026.why: Fastest hosted inference with real no-card free tier
agent-era-2026.review: Free tier no card: 30 RPM, 14,400 RPD, 500K tokens/day. Llama 3.3 70B at 276 t/s.
